summary: vite-plugin-pwa typosquat fetches and executes remote JavaScript via jsonkeeper.com
details: vite-config-field impersonates vite-plugin-pwa: it re-exports the same API names (VitePWA, cachePreset, defaultInjectManifestVitePlugins) and ships a README copied from vite-plugin-pwa, directing users to call configField({}). Invoking configField triggers getUseropt, which uses child_process.spawn('node', ['./client/dev/reactopt.js',...], {detached: true, stdio: 'ignore'}) and unrefs the child. reactopt.js overrides the local process binding with a hardcoded shim whose DEV_API_KEY is https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/J7GQS and DEV_SECRET_KEY/VALUE supply an x-secret-key header. The child performs axios.get against that anonymous mutable JSON paste endpoint and executes the returned document's Cookie field via new Function('require', s)(require), with a 5-attempt retry — arbitrary remote code execution inside the consumer's Node process with full require access. The cover-story naming (reactopt.js under client/dev, faked process.env shim) and typosquat of vite-plugin-pwa confirm supply-chain attack intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
